topics_flat: Algebra > Algebraic Expressions > Sequences and Series > Recurrence relations
Algebra > Algebraic Expressions > Sequences and Series > Sums and products
prompt: Let $(a_n)_{n \ge 1}$ be the sequence defined by $a_1 = 1$ and $a_{n+1} = \frac{a_n}{1+\sqrt{1+a_n}}$, for any $n \in \mathbb{N}^*$. Show that $\lim_{n \to \infty} \frac{a_n}{a_{n+1}} = \lim_{n \to \infty} \sum_{k=1}^{n} \log_2(1+a_k) = 2$. Traian Tămâian
response_text:
Let  
\[
a_1=1,\qquad a_{n+1}=\frac{a_n}{1+\sqrt{1+a_n}}\quad(n\ge 1).
\]

We must show
\[
\lim_{n\to\infty}\frac{a_n}{a_{n+1}}=2
\qquad\text{and}\qquad
\lim_{n\to\infty}\sum_{k=1}^n \log_2(1+a_k)=2.
\]

---

## 1) First limit

From the recurrence,
\[
\frac{a_n}{a_{n+1}}=1+\sqrt{1+a_n}.
\]
So it is enough to prove \(a_n\to 0\).

Since \(a_1=1>0\), all \(a_n>0\). Also
\[
a_{n+1}=\frac{a_n}{1+\sqrt{1+a_n}}<a_n,
\]
so \((a_n)\) is positive and decreasing, hence converges to some \(\ell\ge 0\).

Passing to the limit in
\[
a_{n+1}=\frac{a_n}{1+\sqrt{1+a_n}},
\]
we get
\[
\ell=\frac{\ell}{1+\sqrt{1+\ell}}.
\]
Thus either \(\ell=0\), or \(1+\sqrt{1+\ell}=1\), impossible. Hence \(\ell=0\).

Therefore
\[
\lim_{n\to\infty}\frac{a_n}{a_{n+1}}
=\lim_{n\to\infty}\bigl(1+\sqrt{1+a_n}\bigr)
=1+\sqrt{1+0}=2.
\]

---

## 2) Sum of the logarithms

A key algebraic simplification is
\[
a_{n+1}
=\frac{a_n}{1+\sqrt{1+a_n}}
=\frac{a_n(\sqrt{1+a_n}-1)}{(1+\sqrt{1+a_n})(\sqrt{1+a_n}-1)}
=\sqrt{1+a_n}-1.
\]
Hence
\[
1+a_{n+1}=\sqrt{1+a_n},
\]
so
\[
\log_2(1+a_{n+1})=\frac12\log_2(1+a_n).
\]
Let
\[
b_n=\log_2(1+a_n).
\]
Then
\[
b_{n+1}=\frac12 b_n,\qquad b_1=\log_2(2)=1.
\]
So
\[
b_n=\left(\frac12\right)^{n-1}.
\]

Therefore
\[
\sum_{k=1}^n \log_2(1+a_k)
=\sum_{k=1}^n b_k
=\sum_{k=1}^n \left(\frac12\right)^{k-1}
=2\left(1-\left(\frac12\right)^n\right).
\]
Taking \(n\to\infty\),
\[
\lim_{n\to\infty}\sum_{k=1}^n \log_2(1+a_k)=2.
\]

Thus both limits are equal to \(2\).

Final answer: \(\displaystyle \lim_{n\to\infty}\frac{a_n}{a_{n+1}}=2\) and \(\displaystyle \lim_{n\to\infty}\sum_{k=1}^n \log_2(1+a_k)=2.\)
